name: Roasted Red Pepper and Black Olive Bread
recipeIngredient: 3 Strips Bacon, drained and coarsely chopped
1 medium Onion, peeled, halved and sliced 1/8 inch thick
1/2 cup Nicoise Olives, halved and pitted
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly spray a ripe red pepper with cooking spray and rub it around with your finger to coat the entire surface. Roast the pepper directly in the flames of a gas stove, turning them with tongs until the entire outside of the pepper is blackened. Do not cook the peppers too much; do this as quickly as your range will allow.
2. In a large frying pan or skillet, cook the bacon over medium heat until browned and crispy. Remove the bacon, drain on paper towels, and coarsely chop. Set aside. Add the onion to the reserved bacon drippings along with the olive oil. Stir to coat the onions evenly with the oil. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the onions are a golden brown, about 20 minutes.
3. NOTE: It is important that the onions get browned and that most of the liquid is fried out.
4. Add the garlic and cook for 5 minutes more. Stir in the salt, pepper, roasted red peppers, black olives, and chopped rosemary. Stir in the crumbled bacon. Set aside.
5. In a food processor bowl, combine 3 1/2 cups flour and the sauteed onion/red pepper mixture. Add the yeast, eggs, and parmesan cheese. Combine thoroughly.
6. With the machine running, slowly add water until the mixture begins to pull together and forms a ball. Note: It will not be a rounded ball of dough.
7. If the dough is too sticky, add the remaining flour 1/4 cup at a time. Process until the dough cleans the sides of the bowl, then knead for approximately 1 minute longer.
8. The dough should be smooth and elastic and bounce back when pressed with your finger. Place the dough into a lightly oiled freezer-type plastic bag and let it double in volume in a warm place, about 1 hour (use one gallon bag per loaf; freeze one and continue with the other).
9. Remove the dough from the bag and punch down. Shape the dough into two oblong loaves and place on a lightly greased baking sheet dusted with cornmeal.
10. Make 3 or 4 decorative slashes in the top of the bread and brush with melted butter. Allow to rise again, in a warm, draft-free area until doubled, about 45 minutes.
11. Place the dough into a preheated 375°F oven and bake for 35-45 minutes or until the bread is golden and the bottom sounds hollow when tapped with your finger.
12. Place the bread directly on a wire rack to cool. This bread freezes well for 2-3 months if tightly wrapped. To serve, thaw overnight in foil. To warm, place the thawed bread still wrapped in foil in a 350°F oven for 15-20 minutes. If reheated this way, the bread will retain its freshly baked qualities.
